I INTRODUCTION 
I.1 Product name  
Product name Dataset name 
OMI_HEALTH_CHL_GLOBAL_OceanColour_trend omi_health_chl_global_oceanclour_trend 
I.2 Indicator evaluation method 
The CMEMS Ocean Colour OMIs are derived from the reprocessed (MY) regional gridded 
chlorophyll products generated and distributed by the CMEMS OC -TAC. The input dataset are 
the spectral remote sensing reflectances (Rrs) provided by the ESA Ocean Colour Cli mate 
Change Initiative (ESA OC-CCI, Sathyendranath et al. 2019; Jackson 2020). 
The MY chlorophyll dataset was used to compute the anomaly map, with respect to reference 
climatology (1997-2014), and trend map. 
Trend map was obtained by applying the Census-I algorithm as described by Vantrepotte et al. 
(2009), where the time series is decomposed as a fixed seasonal cycle plus a linear trend 
component plus a residual component.  The linear trend is expressed in % year -1, and its level 
of significance (p) calculated using a t-test. Only significant trends (p < 0.05) are included. 
Product: OMI_HEALTH_CHL_GLOBAL_OceanColour_trend 
This product includes the trend map obtained by using the trend detection method is based 
on the Census -I algorithm as described by Vantrepotte et al. (2009), where the time series is 
decomposed as a fixed seasonal cycle plus a linear trend component plus a residual 
component. The linear trend is expressed in % year -1, and its level of significance (p) 
calculated using a t-test. Only significant trends (p < 0.05) are included. 
Product: OMI_HEALTH_CHL_GLOBAL_OceanColour_area_averaged_anomaly 
The global annual chlorophyll anomaly is computed by subtracting a reference climatology  
(1997-2014) from the annual chlorophyll mean, on a pixel-by-pixel basis and in log10 space.
